text.title: ai-assisted-dev Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-assisted-dev as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: "Prompt and publish" pattern: accepting AI output without review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leads to 4x higher defect rate (per 2025 research cited in README).

failure-mode.description: AI test generation that produces tests passing trivially.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Human must verify test assertions are meaningful; tests may be checking mocks, not behavior.

failure-mode.description: Adopting self-healing test tools without governance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Self-healing test tools (Virtuoso, mabl) introduce non-determinism: tests may silently change meaning when UI changes are detected.

failure-mode.description: Providing vague prompts without architecture context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI tools don't have access to internal architecture context unless explicitly provided via CLAUDE.md / system prompts; vague prompts produce generic code.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When Claude Code + Cursor + Copilot operate simultaneously, each tool sees different history and produces conflicting outputs.
